_comment: Per-city demand modifiers + heat (cop pressure) + OD baselines. Real Appalachia HIDTA / CDC pattern: opioid+meth crisis, weed broadly available, cocaine concentrated in metros. Numbers are gameplay multipliers, NOT clinical statistics.
_seeded_from: DEA National Drug Threat Assessment 2024 — Appalachia HIDTA priorities
CDC WONDER county-level overdose deaths by substance
Ohio Dept of Health overdose dashboards
_demand_multiplier_meaning: 1.0 = national baseline. 1.5 = 50% above baseline (more demand → higher prices, faster sell-through). 0.7 = 30% below.
_heat_meaning: 0-100 cop pressure level. Drives bust frequency, evidence accumulation rate, undercover encounter chance.
_od_baseline_meaning: Annual OD deaths per 100k (rough scale, drives news-feed events and NPC effects).

steubenville_oh._note: Rust Belt blue-collar small city. Opioid crisis hard. Very high fentanyl + meth demand. Less interest in psychedelics.

pittsburgh_pa._note: Major metro. Diverse demand profile, higher heat. University population pulls up MDMA/psychedelics.

wheeling_wv._note: WV opioid epicenter pattern. Highest fentanyl demand in test region.

morgantown_wv._note: WVU college town — psychedelic + cannabis pull. Still significant opioid presence.

columbus_oh._note: OSU + state capital + large metro. High heat — both city PD and state-level investigators.

cleveland_oh._note: Major Lake Erie hub. Heavy cocaine + opioid trafficking corridor.
